content:
metadata:
  version: 1
  generated_from: skeleton.md
stages:
- id: stage-1
  title: Configuration Crystallization
  description: '`Terminus2.__init__` turns ~25 user-supplied configuration parameters (model name, parser choice, max episodes, summarization toggle/threshold, tmux pane dimensions, trajectory config, llm backend kwargs, тАж) into the agent''s immutable instance state. Runs once per agent lifetime, before any task. Sub-steps in source order: handle deprecated `episodes` / `max_episodes` kwargs and resolve `max_episodes` precedence; initialize `_llm` via `_init_llm` (LiteLLM-backed Chat client); pick the parser via `_get_parser` based on `_parser_name`; load the prompt + timeout templates from disk; declare every per-run register at its default (trajectory steps, pending completion, pending subagent refs, pending handoff prompt, asciinema markers, subagent metrics, api request times, summarization count, n_episodes, session id).'
  parent: null
  children: []
- id: stage-2
  title: Environment Setup
  description: '`Terminus2.setup(environment)` instantiates a `TmuxSession` bound to the trial environment (with optional asciinema recording paths derived from `environment.trial_paths`) and starts it. Runs once per trial, after `setup()` is invoked by the Harbor orchestrator but before `run()`. The tmux pane size, extra env, and default user all come from constructor state crystallized in stage-1.'
  parent: null
  children: []
- id: stage-3
  title: Run Onset
  description: The pre-loop portion of `Terminus2.run()`. Reset per-run accumulators via `_reset_per_run_state` (wipes trajectory steps, api request times, n_episodes, summarization count, subagent metrics + rollout details, pending completion / pending subagent refs / pending handoff prompt, timestamped markers; rotates session_id). Construct a fresh `Chat(self._llm, interleaved_thinking=...)`. Capture the initial terminal screen via `session.get_incremental_output()`. Augment the instruction with the MCP servers description (if any) and the dynamically built skills section (`_build_skills_section`). Format the initial prompt via `_prompt_template.format(instruction=..., terminal_state=...)`. Push the initial prompt as trajectory step 1 (`source="user"`). Then enter the iteration loop wrapped in a `try`/`finally`.
  parent: null
  children: []
- id: stage-4
  title: Iteration Loop тШЕ Core тШЕ
  description: '`_run_agent_loop` тАФ the agent''s main work loop. Runs `for episode in range(self._max_episodes)`. Each iteration is decomposed into 10 ordered in-iter steps (see sub-stages). Termination: confirmed-twice task completion (return from the gate), session death (early return on `is_session_alive() == False`), or exhausting `_max_episodes` (loop falls through). The cross-iteration state machine is carried by three registers: `reg-pending-completion` (two-step completion confirmation), `reg-pending-handoff-prompt` and `reg-pending-subagent-refs` (both set either by stage-4.2''s proactive probe OR by `_query_llm`''s reactive ContextLengthExceeded fallback, consumed by stages 4.5 / 4.6 in the next iteration).'
  parent: null
  children:
  - stage-4.1
  - stage-4.2
  - stage-4.3
  - stage-4.4
  - stage-4.6
  - stage-4.8
  - stage-4.9
  - stage-4.10
  - subsys-parser-internal
- id: stage-4.1
  title: Loop Entry Gates
  description: First two operations of each iteration. Bump `self._n_episodes = episode + 1` (1-based for user-facing telemetry). Then `await self._session.is_session_alive()` тАФ on `False`, log "Session has ended" and `return` from the loop entirely (no further iterations, the `finally` in `run()` still fires). This is the only non-completion early-exit path inside the loop.
  parent: stage-4
  children: []
- id: stage-4.2
  title: Proactive Summarize Probe
  description: Only entered when `original_instruction` is set AND `self._enable_summarize` is True. Calls `_check_proactive_summarization(chat, original_instruction, session)`, which compares `context_limit - _count_total_tokens(chat)` against `self._proactive_summarization_threshold`; if below, runs the full `_summarize` side flow (see `side-S1`) and returns `(handoff_prompt, subagent_refs)`. On a non-None return, store the result into `reg-pending-handoff-prompt` and `reg-pending-subagent-refs` so stages 4.5 / 4.6 of the CURRENT iteration can record them in the trajectory.
  parent: stage-4
  children: []
- id: stage-4.3
  title: LLM Query
  description: 'Setup episode-specific logging paths via `_setup_episode_logging`, snapshot pre-call totals (`tokens_before_input/output/cache`, `cost_before`), then invoke `_handle_llm_interaction` тЖТ `_query_llm`. The latter has three exception paths: (a) `ContextLengthExceededError` triggers `_unwind_messages_to_free_tokens(target=4000)` then a three-tier fallback summarize (full `_summarize` тЖТ short summary one-shot LLM тЖТ ultimate "no-LLM" prompt with terminal tail); pending registers are written from this reactive path too. (b) `OutputLengthExceededError` tries `parser.salvage_truncated_response` (XML parser only); if salvage fails, emit a self-explanatory error message to the LLM and recurse. (c) Any other exception is retried up to 3 times by `@retry` (tenacity).'
  parent: stage-4
  children: []
- id: stage-4.4
  title: Response Parse
  description: '`self._parser.parse_response(llm_response.content)` produces `ParseResult(commands, is_task_complete, error, warning, analysis, plan)`. `_handle_llm_interaction` then maps `error` / `warning` into a single `feedback` string with `"ERROR: ..."` and/or `"WARNINGS: ..."` markers (consumed by stages 4.7 and 4.8). Each `ParsedCommand` is wrapped into a `Command(keystrokes, duration_sec=min(duration, 60))`. The parser is chosen at construction time (`subsys-parser-json` or `subsys-parser-xml`) and includes auto-correction for incomplete JSON / mixed content (JSON) or missing closing tags / truncation salvage (XML).'
  parent: stage-4
  children: []
- id: stage-4.6
  title: Pending Handoff Prompt тЖТ User Step (or Split)
  description: 'If `reg-pending-handoff-prompt` is non-None, branch on `self._linear_history`. (a) Linear-history mode: call `_split_trajectory_on_summarization(handoff_prompt)` тАФ does NOT add a step here; instead the split mechanism cuts the current trajectory at the summarization boundary and starts a fresh continuation trajectory file in which the handoff prompt is implicit. (b) Non-linear mode: append `Step(source="user", message=handoff_prompt)` to the live trajectory. Either way, clear the register afterward.'
  parent: stage-4
  children: []
- id: stage-4.8
  title: Error Branch
  description: If `feedback` contains `"ERROR:"`, the iteration aborts the command-execution path. Rebuild `prompt` as a "previous response had parsing errors; please fix" message that includes the feedback and the parser-specific response type label (`_get_error_response_type`). Compute per-step metrics (`cache_tokens_used`, `step_cost`) and append `Step(source="agent", message=llm_response.content, observation.results=[ObservationResult(content=prompt)], metrics=Metrics(...))` тАФ note that for errors we still use the RAW LLM content as the trajectory message (not the structured analysis/plan), since parsing failed. Then `continue` тАФ skipping command execution, completion gate, and loop control for this iteration. Stages 4.9 and 4.10 are NOT reached.
  parent: stage-4
  children: []
- id: stage-4.9
  title: Command Execute тЖТ Observation тЖТ Trajectory Step
  description: 'The normal-path body. (a) `_execute_commands(commands, session)` тЖТ `(timeout_occurred, terminal_output)`; each command is `await session.send_keys(keystrokes, block=False, min_timeout_sec=duration)`. On `TimeoutError` it returns the `_timeout_template`-formatted message. (b) Save `was_pending_completion = self._pending_completion` before mutating it. Build `observation`: three branches тАФ confirmed-complete (uses raw `terminal_output`), first-time `is_task_complete` (sets `reg-pending-completion=True` and returns the `_get_completion_confirmation_message` prompt asking the agent to confirm), and the normal case (`reg-pending-completion=False`; the observation is `_limit_output_length(terminal_output)` optionally prefixed with `"WARNINGS: ..."`). (c) Build `tool_calls`: in raw mode just observation; otherwise one `ToolCall(function_name="bash_command", arguments={keystrokes, duration})` per parsed command, plus a single `ToolCall(function_name="mark_task_complete")` if the agent claimed completion. (d) Append the agent step with `message=message_content` and metrics computed from the pre-call snapshot. (e) `_dump_trajectory()` writes the trajectory JSON to disk so a mid-run crash doesn''t lose progress.'
  parent: stage-4
  children: []
- id: stage-4.10
  title: Completion Gate + Loop Control
  description: End of the iteration body. If `is_task_complete` AND `was_pending_completion` (the agent confirmed completion for the SECOND consecutive turn), `return` тАФ the task is done. If `is_task_complete` but this is the first confirmation, set `prompt = observation` (which is the confirmation question from stage-4.9) and `continue`. Otherwise (normal path), set `prompt = observation` (terminal output) and let the loop continue to the next iteration. The two-step confirmation is the design defense against the agent prematurely claiming completion.
  parent: stage-4
  children: []
- id: stage-5
  title: Run Teardown
  description: The `finally` clause of `Terminus2.run()`. Runs regardless of how the loop ended (normal return, early session-death return, raised exception). Captures `context.rollout_details` by concatenating the main chat's rollout details with `_subagent_rollout_details`. Captures `context.n_input_tokens / n_output_tokens / n_cache_tokens` as the sum of main-chat totals and `_subagent_metrics` totals (subagent token usage is tracked separately throughout the run; this is where it's folded into the trial-level totals). Captures `context.cost_usd` (None when тЙд 0). Writes `context.metadata = {n_episodes, api_request_times_msec, summarization_count}` plus optional `all_messages` for `_store_all_messages`. Finally calls `_dump_trajectory()` so the on-disk trajectory reflects the very last state.
  parent: null
  children: []
- id: stage-6
  title: Recording Post-process (External)
  description: 'Asciinema cast file post-processing тАФ merging the `reg-asciinema-markers` accumulated during the run into the `.cast` recording file. Owned by `AsciinemaHandler.merge_markers()` (see `subsys-asciinema`): preserves the cast header, walks the recording line by line via `_process_recording_line`, and inserts marker events at the right timestamp before each frame whose timestamp тЙе marker time; remaining markers are appended at end via `_write_remaining_markers`. **Not triggered by Terminus 2 directly.** The Harbor framework calls `TmuxSession.stop()` after the agent returns, and that is what invokes the merge тАФ so this stage runs OUTSIDE the agent''s `run()` call.'
  parent: null
  children: []
- id: side-S1
  title: Context Summarization
  description: '`_summarize` тАФ a three-subagent ritual that compresses chat history into a handoff prompt. **Two trigger paths**, both feeding the same routine: (a) PROACTIVE тАФ `_check_proactive_summarization` (stage-4.2) fires when remaining context tokens fall below `_proactive_summarization_threshold`; (b) REACTIVE тАФ inside `_query_llm`''s `ContextLengthExceededError` handler (stage-4.3), after `_unwind_messages_to_free_tokens` frees room, `_summarize` runs as the first of a three-tier fallback (short LLM-call summary, then ultimate no-LLM fallback). **The three subagents**: (1) Summary Generation тАФ sees unwound `chat.messages`, asked for a detailed narrative covering Major Actions / Important Information / Challenging Problems / Current Status; (2) Question Asking тАФ fresh empty history, sees original task + summary + current terminal screen, asked to produce тЙе5 clarifying questions about info missing from the summary; (3) Answer Providing тАФ sees unwound `chat.messages` PLUS the summary prompt/response, asked to answer each question. **Bookkeeping**: each subagent goes through `_run_subagent` which writes its own trajectory file via `_save_subagent_trajectory` and `_append_subagent_response_step`, and yields a `SubagentTrajectoryRef`. **Final state mutation**: `chat._messages = [system, question_prompt, model_questions]` (a fresh 3-message conversation); the answers become the `handoff_prompt` returned to the caller and ultimately surface in the trajectory via `reg-pending-handoff-prompt`. Each invocation increments `reg-summarization-count`.'
  parent: null
  children: []
- id: crosscut-X1
  title: Token & Cost Accounting
  description: Pervasive metric accumulation alongside every LLM call. `_count_total_tokens` (sum of message tokens in a chat), `_track_api_request_time` (append elapsed ms to `reg-api-request-times`), `_extract_usage_metrics` and `_update_subagent_metrics` (fold subagent usage into `reg-subagent-metrics`), `_collect_subagent_rollout_detail` (when `_collect_rollout_details` is on, push full rollout records to `_subagent_rollout_details`). The in-loop pattern `tokens_before_* = chat.total_*_tokens` (snapshot pre-call) тЖТ call тЖТ `cache_tokens_used = chat.total_cache_tokens - tokens_before_cache` тЖТ write to `Step.metrics` is repeated for both error-path and normal-path step construction (stages 4.8 and 4.9), and the final flush into `context.n_*` happens at stage-5. Subagent totals (`reg-subagent-metrics`) are tracked separately so the trial-level totals at stage-5 sum BOTH main chat AND subagents.
  parent: null
  children: []
- id: crosscut-X2
  title: Output Length Limiting
  description: '`_limit_output_length(output, max_bytes=10000)` is the single function used everywhere terminal output reaches the LLM: initial terminal-state capture at stage-3, observation construction at stage-4.9 (both warning and normal paths), and the timeout-template formatting inside `_execute_commands`. Caps the byte size and inserts a `[OUTPUT TRUNCATED]` marker. Not a stage in its own right; called by stage-3 / stage-4.9 / `subsys-tmux` consumers.'
  parent: null
  children: []
- id: crosscut-X3
  title: Logging
  description: 'Debug / warning / error logging via `self.logger` (inherited from `BaseAgent`). Pervasive: setup paths (stage-1/-2), every exception handler in `_query_llm` (stage-4.3), proactive summarize trigger (stage-4.2), subagent ritual progress markers (`side-S1`), session-death message (stage-4.1), max-turns warning at construction (stage-1). Not part of any primary narrative; documented here to explain why nearly every other stage''s source lines include a `self.logger.X(...)` call.'
  parent: null
  children: []
- id: subsys-parser-internal
  title: Parser internal helpers
  description: Internal helper routines inside the parser subsystem that support response parsing after the loop hands off control to the parser. The public parse entrypoint and any directly loop-interpreted parse-result boundary logic should remain in stage-4.4; only implementation-detail helpers belong here.
  parent: stage-4
  children: []
- id: subsys-tmux-internal
  title: Tmux internal helpers
  description: Implementation-detail helpers inside the tmux session subsystem that support environment/session setup and command execution. Public orchestration-facing tmux methods used directly by Terminus2.setup(...) and the stage-4.9 execution path should stay in their consuming flow stages; only lower-level internals should be classified here.
  parent: null
  children: []
state_registers:
- id: reg-pending-completion
  semantics: 'Two-step completion confirmation state machine: set True at the FIRST `is_task_complete`, read at the SECOND to actually return; cross-iteration; written by stage-4.9, read by stages 4.9 (snapshot to `was_pending_completion`) and 4.10 (gate)'
- id: reg-pending-handoff-prompt
  semantics: Summary-handoff prompt produced mid-iteration by `side-S1` (via proactive probe at stage-4.2 OR reactive fallback in stage-4.3); consumed by stage-4.6 to either append a user step or trigger a linear-history split
- id: reg-pending-subagent-refs
  semantics: Subagent trajectory references (one per summarization subagent) to record under a "system" trajectory step; set by `side-S1` via the same two trigger paths as `reg-pending-handoff-prompt`; consumed by stage-4.5
- id: reg-n-episodes
  semantics: Current iteration count (1-based); incremented at stage-4.1; surfaced in `context.metadata` at stage-5
- id: reg-summarization-count
  semantics: Cumulative summarization-ritual invocations; incremented by `_summarize` (side-S1); surfaced in `context.metadata` at stage-5
- id: reg-trajectory-steps
  semantics: Sequenced trajectory of `Step` records (the canonical record of the trial); appended by stages 4.5 / 4.6 / 4.8 / 4.9, written to disk by `_dump_trajectory` at stage-4.9 / stage-5; reset by `_reset_per_run_state` at stage-3
- id: reg-chat-messages
  semantics: Live conversation history sent to LLM (`chat.messages` / `chat._messages`); the summarization side flow REPLACES this with a 3-message [system, question_prompt, model_questions] sequence
- id: reg-asciinema-markers
  semantics: Accumulated `(timestamp, label)` tuples written by `_record_asciinema_marker` at stage-4.7; consumed by `stage-6` via the framework-triggered `TmuxSession.stop()`
- id: reg-subagent-metrics
  semantics: Separate `SubagentMetrics` accumulator for subagent token/cost (kept independent so main-chat metrics stay clean); folded into trial totals at stage-5
- id: reg-api-request-times
  semantics: List of per-LLM-call elapsed milliseconds; appended inside `_query_llm`'s try-blocks (stage-4.3) and `_track_api_request_time`; surfaced in `context.metadata` at stage-5
subsystems:
- id: subsys-tmux
  role: 'Terminal I/O channel between agent and remote environment. `TmuxSession`: `start` (install tmux + asciinema if missing, create session), `stop` (closes session AND triggers `subsys-asciinema` merge), `send_keys` (blocking / non-blocking with key splitting + escaping), `capture_pane` and `get_incremental_output` (terminal state for the prompt), `is_session_alive` (used by stage-4.1 gate)'
- id: subsys-parser-json
  role: 'Parse JSON-formatted LLM responses. `TerminusJSONPlainParser.parse_response` returns `ParseResult(commands, is_task_complete, error, warning, analysis, plan)`. Auto-corrections: incomplete-JSON repair, mixed-content extraction; field-order check is a warning rather than an error'
- id: subsys-parser-xml
  role: 'Parse XML-formatted LLM responses. Same `parse_response` shape. Auto-corrections: missing `<response>` tag wrapping, section-order check; ADDITIONALLY exposes `salvage_truncated_response` consumed by `_query_llm`''s OutputLengthExceededError path at stage-4.3'
- id: subsys-asciinema
  role: 'Post-process: `AsciinemaHandler.merge_markers` inserts the accumulated `reg-asciinema-markers` into the cast file. Triggered by `subsys-tmux.stop()`, which is called by the Harbor framework AFTER `Terminus2.run()` returns тАФ so this subsystem is the worker behind `stage-6`'
- id: subsys-llm
  role: Actual LLM API invocation; not part of this codebase but the primary external dependency. Reached via `chat.chat(...)` (main chat) and `self._llm.call(...)` (short-summary fallback in stage-4.3). Exceptions surface as `ContextLengthExceededError` / `OutputLengthExceededError` / generic Exception, each with its own handling path
